FULL CONTENT:
# [GER 199: Digital Humanities Emblematica Online](https://emblematicaonlineuiuc.wordpress.com/ "GER 199: Digital Humanities Emblematica Online")

![](https://emblematicaonlineuiuc.wordpress.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/12/cropped-actual-final.jpg)

## [Mission Statement](https://emblematicaonlineuiuc.wordpress.com/2013/12/04/mission-statement-2/)

This undergraduate research opportunity received funding from the [School for Literature, Cultures and Linguistics at the University of Illinois at Urbana Champaign.](http://www.slcl.illinois.edu)

Emblem Scholars are Undergraduate Researchers associated with Emblematica Online at the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign. They create metadata by transcribing emblem mottos and associating this data with their respective emblems in their book for scholarly and pedagogical purposes. This digital humanities project focuses on Renaissance Emblem books.

[Leave a comment](https://emblematicaonlineuiuc.wordpress.com/2013/12/04/mission-statement-2/#respond)

enriched_points.expanded_content: Emblematica Online centers on the digitization and study of Renaissance emblem books, showcasing their significance in visual and textual culture during the period.
The project consolidates major collections of emblem books into a single digital platform, facilitating access to 1,388 unique facsimiles from various libraries.
Emblematica Online utilizes interactive features, encouraging users to explore emblems, contribute interpretations, and participate in digital scholarship, enhancing the learning experience.
The initiative fosters collaboration between librarians and scholars to create metadata, ensuring accurate representation and enriching the digital resource for research and education.
